description:
Goal
Pin the final runtime-demand and shared-boundary-transport model in fixtures, telemetry assertions, and docs, then remove construction scaffolding and obsolete expectations.

Problem being fixed
The dump pipeline is useful but janky and old-world-oriented. Runtime-demand correctness should be checked through fixtures2 intent and event-based numbers where possible: functions processed, bodies emitted, instructions, demand counts, omitted lanes, direct-callable transport counts, first-class callable materialization counts, and callable-entry publication counts. This cleanup ticket must reflect the final model, including the fact that direct-only callables can stay direct across return/resume boundaries and therefore some old native callable-boundary expectations are no longer correct.

Code seams
- fixtures2 and compiler2 drive tests that exercise fz2 via filename engine selection
- existing --log-telemetry support in the fz2 compiler
- .agent/docs.md, .agent/strategies.md, and any user-facing guides touched by the runtime-demand / shared transport model
- tests whose old-world assertions still assume zero-capture lambda constructors or returned direct callables must publish native callable-boundary inventory even when demand never made them first-class

Required test intent
- unused callable constructor: exact semantic callable evidence may exist, but no representation work is emitted without demand
- direct lambda call: exact resolved surface becomes direct demand, not first-class closure allocation
- direct callable across return/resume: a direct-only callable can cross a continuation boundary without collapsing into a runtime closure object
- escaped lambda: first-class callable entries are emitted exactly because escape demand exists
- opaque callable: opaque demand is explicit and minimal, not recovered from all type surfaces
- quicksort: tuple-field return/input demand remains the canonical proof that structural demand avoids whole-value over-allocation

Telemetry contract
Stabilize fz.compiler2.runtime_demand.defined or its final name with counts sufficient for tests to assert intent without brittle source dumps. Prefer event counts over fixed text sidecars when they express the semantic invariant better. The final signal should distinguish at least:
- omitted lanes
- tuple-field transport
- direct-callable transport
- first-class callable materializations
- callable-entry publication

Documentation
Update agent docs/guides with the invariant: semantic closure facts are not runtime representation obligations. Representation is derived from runtime demand after semantic closure settles, and one shared boundary-transport model governs inputs, returns, resumes, and captures. Mention where exact callable surfaces live: CallSiteSummary.targets[*].surface_inputs.

Removal target
Remove temporary fixture affordances, TODOs, compatibility switches, stale test assertions tied to the old native callable-boundary model, and any old dump sidecars that no longer express meaningful distinctions. If anything temporary must remain, create a follow-up ticket before closing this one.

Checks
- cargo test --workspace or the agreed fz2 fixture matrix is green except for explicitly authorized unrelated failures
- fz2 --log-telemetry for the pinned fixtures reports the expected demand/materialization/transport counts
- rg confirms removed old-world recovery names are absent or only appear in historical docs/tests intentionally describing the removal
title: Compiler2 runtime demand: pin telemetry, fixtures, docs, and remove scaffolding
